title: Genesis: "I would like to create a faithful javascript port of nethack"
summary: The project begins with a single prompt on a Thursday evening. Within 72 hours: ISAAC64 PRNG ported, C comparison framework built, level generation aligned to 2491 RNG entries, full-screen display comparison working. Multiple agents spawned across quadro simultaneously. The founding insight: if you match every random number, everything else follows.

title: Level Generation Sprint
summary: 126/126 special level files converted. Lua RNG infrastructure for themed rooms. 133 special levels registered. The agents grind through the combinatorial complexity of NetHack's dungeon builder — Sokoban, mines, Oracle, vaults — matching C output room by room. The guidebook gets a polished web version. First signs of the quality gap: tests pass but are they checking the right thing?

title: Test Infrastructure & Metrics
summary: Session recording goes deterministic. The oracle test framework is born: git notes tracking results per commit, class-balanced role matrix benchmarks, backfill scripts. Over 1000 commits already. The human realizes: we need to measure progress systematically, not just accumulate code. Selfplay agents start exploring the dungeon autonomously.

title: The Parity Grind
summary: Deep gameplay parity work: pet retaliation RNG, wizard lifesave, anti-magic traps. Massive surface porting sprint — 73 codematch entries in one day. The watchdog agent gets instructions to nudge other agents toward AGENTS.md. Eat/throw modals, overlay comparison. The human intervenes: 'I hate this — it sounds like a test-only call execution rule whereas the point is supposed to be faithful.' First taste of the complexity cliff.

title: The Silent Week: Autonomous Agents
summary: Five days with zero human messages but 352 commits. The agents work entirely autonomously: Hack 1982 port begins, NetHack HP/PW/regen systems, mkmaze water-level scaffolding, animation system (tmp_at), muse.c parity surfaces. This is the peak of autonomous productivity — and also when quality debt silently accumulates.

title: Iron Parity Campaign
summary: A new strategic phase: the Iron Parity campaign launches with systematic C-style identifier normalization. Translator infrastructure for qsort comparators. Monster movement parity (distfleeck). Then the async revolution: 28 unawaited async calls discovered and fixed in one day. The human starts asking pointed questions about cursor positioning and display parity.

title: Multi-Game Expansion
summary: The project explodes outward. New C runtime sessions recorded. Constants auto-imported from all C headers. Hack 1982 matures with CORR bitfield and step-0 RNG fixes. Then: Rogue 3.6 ported from scratch in a single day — C harness built, function catalog, coverage push. Shell and Logo work begins. The human is now managing a swarm across six games simultaneously.

title: Feature Blitz & Input System
summary: Massive feature implementation: bee_eat, cloneu, petrification (cockatrice!), armoroff, steed system. The input system gets overhauled: centralized --More-- via more(), synclock migration. 44 LORE lessons recorded on March 11 alone — the single biggest learning day. The steed system (mounted combat) is a microcosm of NetHack's complexity: one feature touching everything.

title: Coverage Campaign
summary: Systematic coverage push across all games. Hack mon.js goes from 59% to 99%. Rogue coverage from 84% to 99%. NetHack map-cell caching removes hallucination drift. Dungeon dam lobby navigation. The speedrun walkthrough begins. 37 LORE lessons on March 14. The human drives targeted session design: 'what gameplay actions would exercise the uncovered code?'

title: The Ecosystem: Shell, Logo, Essay
summary: The project becomes a universe. The shell system is built: login prompt, 1982-style chat, scrolling credentials. Logo interpreter created from scratch with a full language specification. The Reflections essay takes shape. Gate 2 screen regression analysis. The human is now simultaneously writing philosophy, building retro computing environments, and debugging C parity.

title: Peak Intensity: seed031 and Unification
summary: The hardest days. 25 LORE lessons on March 22 alone, all on seed031 — one session driving a cascade of discoveries: stale depth caches, aklys stand-off range, chest take-out submenu erasing map rows, resumed meals, pet oeaten timing, bones depth. Session startup unification (Gate 8). The sh interpreter with 166 unit tests. Terminal refactoring unifies all six games. Cursor parity. The project is simultaneously at its most complex and most coherent.
